raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis framing, problem motivation, and contribution claim at the research-writing level.","R1.2":"The draft contains a mathematical argument chain whose assumptions, contraction claim, spectral evidence, and conclusion must cohere.","R1.3":"The empirical convergence, contraction-ratio, mesh-independence, and spectral claims should align with the reported experiments and figures implied by the draft.","R1.5":"A broad improvement request reasonably includes reorganizing section-level presentation so motivation, empirical results, operator definition, theory, and discretization form a clearer narrative arc.","R1.6":"The request explicitly targets a draft, making sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision load-bearing.","RX.1":"Any projection of improvement must preserve uncertainty around empirical estimates, assumptions, and unverified theoretical claims.","RX.2":"The draft makes strong convergence and uniqueness claims, so avoiding overstatement is an active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing experiments, proving new results, or changing the artifact directly.","RX.4":"Because the draft concerns evaluation and calibration protocols, improvements should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unreliable downstream use.","RX.5":"Any suggested responsibility would need to preserve traceability from claims to equations, assumptions, datasets, and reported measurements."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening the thesis framing, problem motivation, and contribution claim at the research-writing level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a mathematical argument chain whose assumptions, contraction claim, spectral evidence, and conclusion must cohere.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical convergence, contraction-ratio, mesh-independence, and spectral claims should align with the reported experiments and figures implied by the draft.
rationale.R1.5: A broad improvement request reasonably includes reorganizing section-level presentation so motivation, empirical results, operator definition, theory, and discretization form a clearer narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: The request explicitly targets a draft, making sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of improvement must preserve uncertainty around empirical estimates, assumptions, and unverified theoretical claims.
rationale.RX.2: The draft makes strong convergence and uniqueness claims, so avoiding overstatement is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing experiments, proving new results, or changing the artifact directly.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft concerns evaluation and calibration protocols, improvements should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unreliable downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: Any suggested responsibility would need to preserve traceability from claims to equations, assumptions, datasets, and reported measurements.
